name: Striploin with Fermented Black Garlic and Red Wine Jus
description: An indulgent fine dining dish featuring buttery Wagyu A5 striploin paired with the deep umami of fermented black garlic and a silky red wine reduction.
keywords: wagyu, beef, black garlic, red wine jus, luxury, fine dining, japanese beef

steps.instruction: Slice the rested Wagyu striploin against the grain into thick medallions.
Spoon a line or dot of black garlic purée onto each plate.
Arrange the sliced beef elegantly beside the purée.
Drizzle red wine jus around the plate for depth and aroma.
Finish with micro greens or chive tips for freshness and contrast.

ingredients.note: trimmed and portioned. A high quality striploin may be used instead of Wagyu.

steps.instruction: Bring the Wagyu to room temperature for 30–45 minutes before cooking.
Season lightly with salt and black pepper — the marbling provides richness, so avoid over-seasoning.
Melt butter, crushed carlic, and thyme over medium heat and keep warm
Heat neutral oil in a heavy pan over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Sear the striploin 45–60 seconds per side for medium-rare, basting with butter, garlic, and thyme during the final moments.
Rest the steak on a rack for 5–7 minutes before slicing.
steps.note: Optionally, pour some of the remaining butter mixture over the steak

steps.instruction: Combine black garlic, olive oil, and a splash of water in a blender.
Blend until silky smooth, adding more liquid as needed for a purée consistency.
Taste and season lightly with salt if needed.
Pass through a fine sieve for an ultra-smooth texture.

ingredients.note: full-bodied, such as Cabernet or Bordeaux

steps.instruction: Sauté shallots and thyme in olive oil over medium heat until soft and lightly caramelized.
Deglaze with red wine and reduce by two-thirds.
Add stock and continue to reduce until thick and glossy.
Strain through a fine sieve and whisk in cold butter to finish the sauce.
Adjust seasoning and keep warm.
